title: Venue & Travel
text: This page brings together practical information to help you plan your visit to Glasgow for the EnviBayes-INLA workshop. Sections marked coming soon will be updated as details are confirmed — check back closer to the event.

title: Venue & Travel
text:

title: Venue & Travel
text:
🏨 Accommodation
The University of Glasgow is situated in the West End, with a variety of small hotels and guest houses nearby, as well as easy links to City Centre hotels via the Subway.
The closest Subway station to the main University campus is Hillhead. For City Centre hotels, the closest stations are Buchanan Street and St. Enoch. The journey between the West End and City Centre takes around 10 minutes by Subway.
Delegates are advised to book early to guarantee availability at their preferred hotel.
 Download Accomodation details PDF

title: Venue & Travel
text:
✈️ Getting to Glasgow
Guidance on arriving by air (Glasgow and Edinburgh airports), rail connections to Glasgow Central and Queen Street stations, and intercity coach services — via VisitGlasgow’s official travel guide.
View full travel guide →

🛂 Visa Information
Delegates travelling from outside the UK should check whether they need a visa or an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) to attend. As of February 2026, most visa-exempt visitors (including from the EU, US, Canada, and Australia) now require an ETA before travelling — this is separate from a visa and is usually approved within a few days, but we recommend applying at least 2–3 weeks before travel. Check requirements via the UK government’s visa/ETA checker.






NoteInvitation letter for visa application



If you require an official letter of invitation to support your visa or ETA application, please get in touch with the organising committee with your name, affiliation, and passport details as they appear on your travel documents.

title: Venue & Travel
text:
🚇 Exploring & Getting Around Glasgow
Glasgow is a vibrant and compact city with plenty to offer delegates outside of conference sessions.
The city has a range of cultural attractions including the works of Charles Rennie Mackintosh and many museums and art galleries offering free entry.
If you’re extending your stay, Glasgow has excellent travel connections for exploring Scotland and an exciting selection of year-round tours leaving daily for the Scottish Highlands.
Delegates can look forward to a warm welcome in a city voted ‘world’s friendliest city’ by Rough Guides.
Plan your trip at: visitglasgow.com
Getting around guide →
Things to do →

title: Workshop Descriptions
text:
The following workshops will be delivered prior to the EnviBayes-INLA workshop on the 21/06/2027 at the School of Mathematics & Statistics.
Example






NoteINLA AND BEYOND: Bayesian Hierarchical Modelling with inlabru




Jafet Belmont Osuna*, University of Glasgow

INLA, a powerful tool for efficiently fitting complex spatial models, comes with a steep learning curve. This workshop introduces inlabru – a user-friendly interface to INLA. Learn to specify models intuitively, handle spatial data efficiently, and focus on data structures and interpretation relevant to ecology, rather than computational complexity!
Duration : 4 hours
Time: TBC
